data.title: Peter Schiff: If the Fed does not urgently cut interest rates and announce a massive quantitative easing program, it could trigger a stock market crash similar to 1987
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Economist Peter Schiff warned that the US Treasury yields are rising rapidly, with the 10-year yield reaching 4.5% and the 30-year yield rising to 5%. He said that if the Federal Reserve does not urgently cut interest rates and launch a large-scale quantitative easing program tomorrow morning, the stock market may experience a crash similar to that of 1987.

data.title: The three major U.S. stock index futures rebounded, and the S&P 500 and Dow futures briefly recovered all their losses
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The three major U.S. stock index futures rebounded, with Nasdaq futures rising 0.1%, and S&P 500 futures and Dow futures briefly recovering all losses. (Jinshi)

data.title: Japan's 30-year bond yield hits 21-year high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Japan's 30-year government bond yields rose to a 21-year high on Wednesday as investors sold the most liquid bonds to raise cash amid a market rout caused by U.S. trade tariffs. As stocks and oil prices plummeted, the 30-year Japanese bond yield rose to as high as 2.785%, the highest level since August 2004, and rose 22 basis points on the day to 2.715%. "The yield on ultra-long bonds has risen to a level higher than before Trump announced the tariff increase, which is like panic selling," said Katsutoshi Inadome, senior strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Asset Management. The Bank of Japan, the Ministry of Finance and the Financial Services Agency will hold a meeting of senior officials from 15:00 Beijing time to discuss financial market issues. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ansem: Market rebound needs specific news support, currently active bottom-fishing is not as good as passive response
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Well-known trader Ansem Yu X wrote that unless there is a specific news event that can change the current situation, whether it is a shift in the Fed or a easing of tariffs, there will be no V-shaped reversal in the current market conditions. For these two situations, it is better to respond passively after the news appears rather than actively predicting the bottom.

data.title: On the first day of the implementation of the US reciprocal tariffs, European stocks opened with a collective decline
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Market data shows that European stock indexes fell more than 3% at the opening, the European Stoxx 50 index fell more than 3%, the German DAX index fell 3%, the British FTSE 100 index fell 2%, the French CAC40 index fell 2%, and the Italian FTSE MIB index fell 3.4%. The US's 20% reciprocal tariff on the EU officially took effect today. (Jinshi)

data.title: Florida's Bitcoin Reserve Bill Set for Public Hearing
data.description: According to Odaily, the Florida Bitcoin Reserve Bill HB 487 is scheduled for a public hearing this Thursday, April 10, in the Insurance and Banking Subcommittee. This marks the first public hearing on the Bitcoin Reserve Bill in Florida since its introduction two months ago.

data.title: Markets fully price in 25bps ECB rate cut in April
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Eurozone money markets are fully pricing in a 25 basis point rate cut by the European Central Bank in April, with data showing an 85% chance of a rate cut by the European Central Bank on Tuesday. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance will delist multiple spot trading pairs on April 11
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance will delist ACT/BRL, ALPHA/BTC, BLUR/BTC, CELR/BTC, PENGU/BNB, POND/BTC, RUNE/BNB and other spot trading pairs at 03:00 (UTC) on April 11, 2025. At the same time, Binance will terminate the spot trading robot service for the above trading pairs. Users can still trade related currencies on other available trading pairs. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Nikkei 225 and KOSPI Indexes Experience Declines
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Nikkei 225 index closed down by 1,298.55 points, a decrease of 3.93%, ending at 31,714.03 points. This follows a gain of over 6% the previous day.

Meanwhile, South Korea's KOSPI index fell by 40.55 points, or 1.74%, closing at 2,293.68 points. This decline comes after a 0.26% increase the day before.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yield Surge Puts Pressure on Risk Assets
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Treasury market is experiencing a wave of 'basis trade' unwinding, leading to a sharp increase in yields. The 10-year Treasury yield has surged to 4.5%, while the 30-year yield is approaching 5%, exacerbating liquidity concerns and putting significant pressure on risk assets.

Analysis firm Hyblock has issued a warning that if Bitcoin's price falls to the range of $73,800 to $74,400, it could trigger a large-scale liquidation of leveraged long positions, intensifying downward volatility. Hyblock also highlighted that there is support near $70,000, and a breach of this level could lead to further stop-losses and liquidations.
